g.aria-label: The login route now validates input, uses proper password hashing instead of plaintext comparison, and sets a secure HTTP cookie. These are fundamental
g.title: The login route now validates input, uses proper password hashing instead of plaintext comparison, and sets a secure HTTP cookie. These are fundamental

g.aria-label: Input validation is a key security practice.
g.title: Input validation is a key security practice.

g.aria-label: Always verify that required fields exist before
g.title: Always verify that required fields exist before

g.aria-label: using them вҖ” this prevents crashes from undefined
g.title: using them вҖ” this prevents crashes from undefined

g.aria-label: values and makes error messages more helpful.
g.title: values and makes error messages more helpful.

g.aria-label: verifyPassword uses bcrypt to compare the
g.title: verifyPassword uses bcrypt to compare the

g.aria-label: plaintext password against a stored hash. The old
g.title: plaintext password against a stored hash. The old

g.aria-label: code compared passwords directly, which means
g.title: code compared passwords directly, which means

g.aria-label: passwords were stored in plaintext вҖ” a critical
g.title: passwords were stored in plaintext вҖ” a critical

g.aria-label: httpOnly prevents JavaScript from reading the
g.title: httpOnly prevents JavaScript from reading the

g.aria-label: it's only sent over HTTPS. 'sameSite: strict'
g.title: it's only sent over HTTPS. 'sameSite: strict'

g.aria-label: prevents the cookie from being sent in cross-site
g.title: prevents the cookie from being sent in cross-site

g.aria-label: A new module that initializes the Redis connection. It reads the connection URL from an environment variable with a sensible local default, and includes retry
g.title: A new module that initializes the Redis connection. It reads the connection URL from an environment variable with a sensible local default, and includes retry

g.aria-label: The nullish coalescing operator (??) returns the right side only if the left is null or undefined. This gives you a
g.title: The nullish coalescing operator (??) returns the right side only if the left is null or undefined. This gives you a

g.aria-label: fallback: use the env variable if set, otherwise default to localhost.
g.title: fallback: use the env variable if set, otherwise default to localhost.

g.aria-label: A retry strategy controls what happens when Redis is temporarily unreachable. This implements exponential backoff вҖ”
g.title: A retry strategy controls what happens when Redis is temporarily unreachable. This implements exponential backoff вҖ”

g.aria-label: waiting longer between each retry, capped at 5 seconds вҖ” to avoid overwhelming a recovering server.
g.title: waiting longer between each retry, capped at 5 seconds вҖ” to avoid overwhelming a recovering server.

g.aria-label: Listening for 'error' events prevents unhandled exceptions from crashing the process. In Node.js, an EventEmitter that
g.title: Listening for 'error' events prevents unhandled exceptions from crashing the process. In Node.js, an EventEmitter that

g.aria-label: emits 'error' with no listener will throw.
g.title: emits 'error' with no listener will throw.
